judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with equivalent depth and accuracy; they are comparable.

report_1.evidence:
A clinic that has never opened the setting WILL have reminders sent automatically.**
The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py`, specifically lines 11–12... so `.get()` returns `None` and line 12 returns `True`.

report_2.evidence: A clinic that has never opened the setting WILL have reminders sent automatically.** The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py`, specifically lines 11–12... `.get()` returns `None` and the function returns `True`.

report_1.evidence: The change: in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` (`feature_flags.py:12`), return `False` when the value is `None`/missing.

report_2.evidence: The change I'd make is in `backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-13`: treat a missing value as disabled

report_1.evidence: For a compact-calendar UI preference, defaulting missing-to-on is a reasonable "grandfather existing tenants in" choice... Copying that resolver shape into a flag that fires automated outbound patient messaging carried the convenience-feature default into a category where the failure mode is silently messaging every existing clinic's patients.

report_2.evidence: Defaulting a *display preference* to on is a reasonable product decision... Copying that default onto a flag that triggers outbound SMS/email to patients transplants the pattern into a context where its consequences are categorically different.

report_1.evidence: Add an env-level master kill switch** ANDed with the per-tenant value in the resolver... Right now the only "off" lever is per-tenant data edits; there is no single switch to stop all sending if the job misbehaves

report_2.evidence: Consider a global kill switch** (env/infra-level) in addition to the per-tenant flag, so the whole feature can be halted quickly if the rollout goes wrong; `infra/main.tf` currently defines only an `environment` variable.
